project: AI Voice Assistant for Amazfit Balance
language: TypeScript (both watch app in app/ and server in server/)
description: AI Voice Assistant for Amazfit Balance - STT/LLM/TTS pipeline with Zepp OS TypeScript app and Node.js/TypeScript server

userStories.title: Set up Zepp OS project from template
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Zepp OS project scaffolded so we can build the watch app.
acceptanceCriteria: Zepp OS project created in app/ directory from official template
Project targets Amazfit Balance (round watch, 480x480)
package.json and app.json configured correctly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: After this story, yield control to the user. They must install Zepp CLI, register a dev account, and verify the app runs in the simulator before continuing.

userStories.title: Watch UI - recording button and state indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want a button on the watch face to start/stop voice recording with visual feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Main page has a prominent record button
Visual indicator shows current state: idle, recording, sending, waiting, playing
Button press toggles recording on/off
Uses Zepp OS Recorder API to capture audio in OPUS format
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: After this story, user must test audio recording and playback on the simulator or real device.

userStories.title: Watch audio playback
userStories.description: As a user, I want recorded audio to play back on the watch so I can verify capture works.
acceptanceCriteria: Recorded OPUS audio plays back through watch speaker via Player API
Playback starts automatically after recording stops (for testing)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: User must verify recording and playback works on simulator/device before continuing.

userStories.title: Build echo server with auth
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Node.js server that echoes back received audio, so I can test the pipeline before adding AI.
acceptanceCriteria: Node.js/Express server in TypeScript in server/ directory
POST /api/ask endpoint accepts audio bytes in request body
Validates shared secret API token from Authorization header
Returns 401 for missing/invalid token
Returns the same audio bytes in response on valid request
Server reads config from environment variables (PORT, API_TOKEN)
package.json with start script
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Side Service - send audio to server
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Side Service to forward recorded audio from the watch to the server and return the response.
acceptanceCriteria: Side Service receives audio data from watch via BLE messaging
Side Service sends audio to server via fetch() POST with Authorization header
Side Service receives server response and forwards audio back to watch
Watch plays received audio via Player API
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: After this story, user must test the full round-trip: watch -> Side Service -> echo server -> Side Service -> watch.

userStories.title: Watch to Side Service BLE communication
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the watch app to send recorded audio to the Side Service and receive audio responses back over BLE.
acceptanceCriteria: Watch sends recorded OPUS audio bytes to Side Service via Messaging or TransferFile API
Watch receives audio response from Side Service
Watch plays received audio via Player API
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: User must test full echo round-trip on simulator and then on real Amazfit Balance device. Report any BLE transfer issues or limitations.

userStories.title: Server - STT via Groq Whisper
userStories.description: As a user, I want my voice transcribed to text so the AI can understand my question.
acceptanceCriteria: Server sends received audio to Groq Whisper API (POST /audio/transcriptions, model: whisper-large-v3)
Uses Groq API key from request header (X-Groq-Key)
Transcribed text extracted from response JSON
Returns error message to client if Whisper API fails
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server - LLM provider abstraction layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a clean abstraction for LLM providers so adding new ones is easy.
acceptanceCriteria: LLM module accepts: provider name, model name, API key, messages array, max turns
Module validates and caps conversation history to max turns (default: 10)
Returns response text from the LLM
Provider interface is simple enough to add new providers with minimal code
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server - Groq LLM provider
userStories.description: As a user, I want to get answers from Groq (default LLM provider).
acceptanceCriteria: Groq provider sends messages to POST /chat/completions
Default model: moonshotai/kimi-k2-instruct
Uses Groq API key from request
Conversation history included in messages array
Response text extracted from API response
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server - Claude LLM provider
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose Claude as my LLM for higher quality answers.
acceptanceCriteria: Claude provider sends messages to Anthropic API
Supports model selection: haiku 4.5, sonnet 4.6
Uses Anthropic API key from request (X-LLM-Key header)
Same conversation history handling as Groq provider
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server - ChatGPT LLM provider
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose ChatGPT as my LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenAI provider sends messages to OpenAI chat completions API
Supports model selection: gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4o
Uses OpenAI API key from request (X-LLM-Key header)
Same conversation history handling as Groq provider
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server - TTS via Groq Orpheus with ffmpeg conversion
userStories.description: As a user, I want the AI response converted to speech audio I can hear on my watch.
acceptanceCriteria: Server sends LLM response text to Groq TTS API (POST /audio/speech, model: canopylabs/orpheus-v1-english)
Voice selection from request (default: austin)
WAV response converted to OPUS via ffmpeg child process
OPUS audio bytes returned in server response
Uses Groq API key from request
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server - wire up full pipeline endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the /api/ask endpoint to chain STT -> LLM -> TTS and return audio + text.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/ask accepts: audio bytes, conversation history, provider config (provider, model, voice, max turns), API keys
Pipeline: receive audio -> Whisper STT -> LLM (selected provider) -> Orpheus TTS -> ffmpeg -> respond
Response includes: OPUS audio, transcribed question text, LLM response text, updated conversation array
Errors at any stage return a meaningful error message to the client
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Client-side conversation memory in Side Service
userStories.description: As a user, I want follow-up questions to have context from previous exchanges.
acceptanceCriteria: Side Service maintains an array of {role, content} message objects
Conversation array sent alongside audio in each POST to server
Side Service updates its array with the conversation returned by server
Array capped to configurable max turns (default: 10) before sending
Conversation resets when the app is closed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DEPENDS ON US-013 response format change: US-013 changes the server response from raw audio bytes to JSON ({ audio, question, answer, conversation }). This story MUST update the Side Service to parse that JSON response — extract the audio bytes for playback and the conversation array for memory. The current side service does response.arrayBuffer() which will break after US-013 lands. ALSO: the server /api/ask endpoint now expects multipart/form-data (not raw binary + headers). The Side Service must build a FormData with two fields: 'audio' (binary OPUS Blob) and 'config' (JSON string with { groqKey, llmProvider, llmModel, llmKey, ttsVoice, maxTurns, conversation }). All the X-* headers are gone.

userStories.title: Watch UI - display question and response text
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my transcribed question and the AI's answer on the watch screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Transcribed user question displayed on screen after server responds
AI response text displayed below the question
Text is scrollable if it exceeds screen size
Loading/status indicators during: recording, sending, waiting, playing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: User must verify UI displays correctly on the watch (text rendering, scrolling, indicators).

userStories.title: Watch settings page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure the assistant settings from my watch or companion app.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page accessible from the app
Configurable: server URL, server API token, Groq API key
Configurable: LLM provider (Groq/Claude/ChatGPT), LLM API key, model selection
Configurable: TTS voice (austin, troy, hannah, etc.), max conversation turns (default: 10)
Model options update based on selected LLM provider
Settings persist across app restarts
App validates required keys are set before allowing voice queries
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: User must verify settings page works and values persist correctly.

userStories.title: Readme, placeholder icon, and deployment readiness
userStories.description: As a developer and end user, I need documentation, a placeholder app icon, and scripts to build and run the project.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md created with user-facing section: what the app does, prerequisites (Groq API key, server URL), setup steps with PLACEHOLDER_SERVER_URL and PLACEHOLDER_API_KEY clearly marked for the user to fill in
README.md includes developer section: how to deploy the server (clone, npm install, environment variables, npm run start), how to build and sideload the watch app, how to contribute
Placeholder icon added at app/assets/icon.png (any solid-color PNG, 240x240 minimum) with a comment in README that it should be replaced
Root package.json has a 'start' script that starts the server (e.g. node server/index.js)
Root package.json has a 'build' script that builds the Zepp OS watch app (e.g. zeus build)
server/package.json (or root) has all server runtime dependencies listed so npm install works out of the box
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: User will replace the placeholder icon with the real one and fill in their server URL and API key in the README examples.

userStories.title: Benchmark ChatGPT and Claude vs Groq
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to benchmark all LLM providers to document speed, accuracy, and cost tradeoffs.
acceptanceCriteria: Test gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4o, haiku 4.5, sonnet 4.6 with identical prompts
Measure response latency, answer accuracy (current events, general knowledge), cost per request
Compare against Groq kimi-k2-instruct baseline (~0.42s)
Results documented in project
userStories.notes: This requires API keys for OpenAI and Anthropic. Yield control to the user to provide keys and review results.

userStories.title: Watch UI - error state with dismissible error screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a clear error message when something goes wrong and be able to dismiss it and return to the home screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Any error from the server (network failure, non-2xx response, STT/LLM/TTS failure) transitions the watch UI to an Error state
Error screen displays the error message text
Tapping anywhere on the error screen dismisses it and returns to Idle state
Error state has a distinct visual style (e.g. red background or icon) to distinguish it from normal states
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HUMAN CHECKPOINT: User must verify the error screen displays correctly and tap-to-dismiss works on the simulator/device.

userStories.title: Code quality: TypeScript strictness, Prettier, and cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the codebase to be clean, strictly typed, and consistently formatted so it is easy to maintain and extend.
acceptanceCriteria: No use of `any` type anywhere in the codebase
No `as X` type casts — use proper type declarations, type guards, or `satisfies` instead
Prettier config added (`.prettierrc`) and all files formatted
All debug `console.log` / `console.error` statements removed or replaced with structured logging where appropriate
No dead code, commented-out blocks, or leftover TODOs
Code is readable: clear variable names, no overly clever one-liners
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Run this story last — after all features are implemented — so refactoring does not conflict with in-progress feature work.
